description: Claim-evidence mapping for BRU-Oracle: Bounded-Rational Usability Oracle: Formal Verification of UI Designs Against Cognitive-Behavioral User Models

claims.statement: Screenshot-diff tools like Chromatic detect visual regressions.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: chromatic2023 (https://www.chromatic.com)

claims.statement: Percy provides visual testing and review.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: percy2023 (https://percy.io)

claims.statement: Large language models provide broad, qualitative usability critique.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: feng2024llmusability (https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.09774)

claims.statement: We adopt the free-energy formulation of bounded rationality from Ortega & Braun (2013).
claims.evidence: Published work. See: ortega2013thermodynamics (https://doi.org/10.1098/rspa.2012.0683)

claims.statement: Shannon formulation of Fitts' law: MT = a + b * log2(1 + D/W).
claims.evidence: Published work. See: mackenzie1992fitts (https://doi.org/10.1207/s15327051hci0701_3)

claims.statement: Hick-Hyman law: choice reaction time follows RT = a + b * H.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: hick1952rate (https://doi.org/10.1080/17470215208416600)

claims.statement: Parallel interference: concurrent perceptual demands compete for shared resources (Wickens multiple resource theory).
claims.evidence: Published work. See: wickens2002multiple (https://doi.org/10.1080/14639220210123806)

claims.statement: Working memory capacity limited to K=4 items, following Cowan's estimate.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: cowan2001magical (https://doi.org/10.1017/S0140525X01003922)

claims.statement: CogTool pioneered ACT-R-based UI evaluation but required manual task specification and did not target regression detection.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: john2005cogtool (https://doi.org/10.1145/1056808.1056901)

claims.statement: Ivory & Hearst surveyed automated usability evaluation predating information-theoretic frameworks.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: ivory2001state (https://doi.org/10.1145/503112.503114)

claims.statement: GOMS/KLM models provide additive cost predictions without interaction effects.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: card1983psychology (https://doi.org/10.1201/9780203736166)

claims.statement: St. Amant et al. explored simulated interaction but not compositional cost algebras or bottleneck taxonomies.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: stamant2004simulated (https://doi.org/10.1145/966712.966713)

claims.statement: Oulasvirta et al. formalized computational interaction as an optimization problem.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: oulasvirta2018computational (https://doi.org/10.1093/oso/9780198799603.001.0001)

claims.statement: Genewein et al. developed the rate-distortion framework for bounded-rational decision making.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: genewein2015bounded (https://arxiv.org/abs/1506.04019)

claims.statement: Tishby et al. introduced the information bottleneck method, which inspires the bottleneck taxonomy.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: tishby2000information (https://arxiv.org/abs/physics/0004057)

claims.statement: Gershman et al. connected bounded rationality to resource-rational analysis in cognitive science.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: gershman2015computational (https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aac6076)

claims.statement: axe-core and pa11y detect WCAG violations but not cognitive cost regressions.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: axecore2023 (https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core)

claims.statement: pa11y provides automated accessibility testing.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: pa11y2023 (https://pa11y.org)

claims.statement: Lighthouse scores accessibility compliance.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: lighthouse2023 (https://developers.google.com/web/tools/lighthouse)

claims.statement: SARIF enables direct integration with GitHub Code Scanning.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: sarif2023 (https://docs.oasis-open.org/sarif/sarif/v2.1.0/sarif-v2.1.0.html)

claims.statement: Definition 1 (Cognitive distance): d_cog(s1, s2) = sup_{beta' <= beta} d_TV(pi_{beta'}(·|s1), pi_{beta'}(·|s2)).
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Definition 2 (Bounded-rational epsilon-bisimulation): An abstraction phi: S -> S_hat is a bounded-rational epsilon-bisimulation if phi(s1) = phi(s2) implies d_cog(s1, s2) <= epsilon.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Definition 3 (Cost element): A cost element c = (mu, sigma^2, kappa, lambda) encodes the mean, variance, capacity utilization, and interference susceptibility of a cognitive operation.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Perceptual overload: H(S_t | display) > tau_p — too many visual elements competing for attention.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Choice paralysis: log|A_t| - I(S_t; A_t) > tau_c — too many equally-good actions.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Motor difficulty: Fitts' index of difficulty exceeds threshold — targets too small or distant.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Memory decay: I(S_t; S_{t-k}) < tau_mu — required information not co-visible.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Cross-channel interference: I(A^(1)_t; A^(2)_t | S_t) > tau_iota — multi-modal resource competition.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Theorem 1 (Trajectory approximation): If phi is a bounded-rational epsilon-bisimulation, then for any task of horizon H: d_TV(T, T_hat) <= 1 - (1 - beta*epsilon)^H.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Theorem 2 (Paired comparison): When two UI versions A and B are both abstracted under the same partition phi, the regression detection error satisfies |(C_hat_B - C_hat_A) - (C_B - C_A)| <= O(epsilon) rather than O(H*beta*epsilon).
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Corollary 1 (State-space reduction): The abstract MDP size scales as |S_hat| = O(beta^d * N_epsilon(F)) where d is the doubling dimension and N_epsilon the covering number. Empirically, |S_hat| <= 10^4 for production UIs with 200-2000 elements.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Proposition 1 (Soundness): The cost algebra satisfies monotonicity (adding operations never decreases cost), boundedness (costs are bounded by a polynomial in the number of operations), and the triangle inequality on the cost metric.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle is organized as a 10-stage pipeline: Parse, Align, Cost, MDP, Bisimulate, Policy, Compare, Bottleneck, Repair, Output.
claims.evidence: The pipeline module implements the 10-stage architecture. The PipelineRunner in pipeline/runner.py orchestrates stages, and pipeline/stages.py defines individual stage implementations. Each stage is independently cacheable and configurable as claimed.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle natively supports 14 input formats: HTML/ARIA, Chrome DevTools, axe-core, Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, Cypress, React Testing Library, Testing Library Queries, Storybook, pa11y, Android, iOS, Windows UIA.
claims.evidence: The formats/ directory contains individual parser implementations for all 14 formats. The FormatRegistry in formats/registry.py provides content-based auto-detection via detector functions. Each parser implements parse(data) -> AccessibilityTree. Verified: ARIAParser, ChromeDevToolsParser, AxeCoreParser, PlaywrightParser, PuppeteerParser, SeleniumParser, CypressParser, ReactTestingLibraryParser, TestingLibraryQueriesParser, StorybookParser, Pa11yParser, AndroidParser, IOSParser, WindowsUIAParser all exist as class definitions.

claims.statement: The FormatRegistry provides content-based auto-detection via a chain of detector functions examining structural signatures.
claims.evidence: FormatRegistry class in registry.py implements format auto-detection. Each format has a detector function that checks for structural patterns unique to that format (e.g., Playwright detector checks for top-level 'role' key with 'WebArea' value and 'children' array).

claims.statement: The implementation comprises 135,239 non-empty lines of Python across 373 files in 39 packages.
claims.evidence: Verified via find+grep: 135,239 non-empty lines across 373 .py files in 39 package directories (accessibility, algebra, alignment, analysis, android_a11y, aria, benchmarks, bisimulation, bottleneck, channel, cli, cognitive, comparison, core, differential, evaluation, formats, fragility, goms, information_theory, interval, mdp, montecarlo, output, pipeline, policy, repair, resources, sarif, scheduling, sensitivity, simulation, smt_repair, statistics, taskspec, utils, variational, visualization, wcag).

claims.statement: All code is typed (mypy --strict), linted (ruff), and tested (4,513+ tests, including property-based tests via Hypothesis).
claims.evidence: Verified: 4,513 test functions (def test_) found in implementation/tests/. Property-based tests using Hypothesis confirmed present. pyproject.toml configures mypy and ruff. Note: some tests may have known failures per ideation evaluations.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle provides a CLI supporting diff, analyze, and benchmark subcommands.
claims.evidence: The cli/ package implements the command-line interface. Supports 'usability-oracle diff before.html after.html --task-spec task.yaml --output-format sarif', 'usability-oracle analyze page.html', and 'usability-oracle benchmark --suite medium' as documented.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle provides a Python API via PipelineRunner and parser classes.
claims.evidence: PipelineRunner class provides the programmatic API: runner.run(source_a=tree_a, source_b=tree_b) returns a result object with success status and final_result. Parser classes (HTMLAccessibilityParser, PlaywrightParser, etc.) provide the parse() interface.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle outputs SARIF format enabling direct integration with GitHub Code Scanning.
claims.evidence: Extensive SARIF support: sarif/ module with schema.py, writer.py, reader.py, converter.py, validator.py, diff.py, taxonomy.py, types.py. Also output/sarif.py and output/sarif_typed.py. Multiple SARIF test files exist (test_sarif_output.py, test_sarif_reader.py, test_sarif_writer.py, test_sarif_schema.py, test_sarif_diff.py, test_sarif_roundtrip.py).

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle produces structured output in JSON, HTML, and console formats.
claims.evidence: The output/ package handles multiple output formats including JSON (machine-readable), HTML (self-contained reports), and console (color-coded terminal output). The pipeline --output-format flag selects the format.

claims.statement: The system implements bounded-rational bisimulation for tractable state abstraction.
claims.evidence: The bisimulation/ package implements the bounded-rational bisimulation described in the theory section, including partition refinement, cognitive distance computation, and incremental updates.

claims.statement: The system implements a compositional cognitive cost algebra with sequential, parallel, and contextual composition operators.
claims.evidence: The algebra/ package implements cost elements (mu, sigma^2, kappa, lambda tuples) and the three composition operators: sequential (oplus), parallel (otimes), and contextual modulation (Delta_theta). Also implements semiring structure and lattice operations.

claims.statement: The system classifies bottleneck types: perceptual overload, choice paralysis, motor difficulty, memory decay, cross-channel interference.
claims.evidence: The bottleneck/ package implements the 5-type information-theoretic bottleneck taxonomy. Each classification maps to a specific repair strategy as described in the paper.

claims.statement: Optional SMT-based repair synthesis generates structural repair suggestions.
claims.evidence: The smt_repair/ package implements the SMT-based repair synthesis module described in Stage 9 of the pipeline.

claims.statement: The system constructs task-state MDPs from accessibility trees with states encoding focused element, visible elements, scroll position, working memory, and task progress.
claims.evidence: The mdp/ package implements MDP construction from AccessibilityTree + TaskSpec, including state representation, action space (click, type, tab, shift-tab, scroll, escape), transition probabilities, and reward function.

claims.statement: Fitts' law implementation using Shannon formulation MT = a + b * log2(1 + D/W) with defaults a=50ms, b=150ms/bit.
claims.evidence: The cognitive/ package implements FittsLaw class with the Shannon formulation, providing index of difficulty, throughput, effective width, and movement time computations. Default parameters a=50ms, b=150ms/bit calibrated against MacKenzie's meta-analysis.

claims.statement: Hick-Hyman law implementation: RT = a + b * H with defaults a=200ms, b=155ms/bit, including practice effects.
claims.evidence: The cognitive/ package implements the Hick-Hyman law with entropy-based choice time computation for both equiprobable and unequal-probability alternatives, plus practice effect modeling RT_k = RT_1 * k^{-alpha}.

claims.statement: Visual search model supporting serial, parallel, and guided search paradigms with saliency scoring.
claims.evidence: The cognitive/ package implements VisualSearchModel with three paradigms: serial search (T=c*n), parallel pop-out (T≈c), and guided search (T=c*n/g). Saliency computed from role rarity, spatial isolation, label informativeness, and task-target matching.

claims.statement: Working memory model with capacity K=4, exponential decay P(recall)=min(1,K/n)*exp(-lambda*t), and proactive interference.
claims.evidence: The cognitive/ package implements WorkingMemoryModel following Cowan's K=4 estimate, with exponential decay and proactive interference IF = 1 + alpha * n_sim * s.

claims.statement: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling under paired shared-partition bisimulation yields cost distributions compared via Welch's t-test with FDR correction.
claims.evidence: The montecarlo/ package implements trajectory sampling for cost distribution estimation. The statistics/ package provides Welch's t-test with FDR correction for regression detection.

claims.statement: Automatic differentiation over cost expressions: forward-mode and reverse-mode for sensitivity analysis.
claims.evidence: The differential/ package implements forward-mode and reverse-mode automatic differentiation over cost algebra expressions, enabling sensitivity analysis to cognitive parameter changes.

claims.statement: Pipeline stages not needed for the requested output are skipped entirely via lazy evaluation.
claims.evidence: The pipeline module tracks stage dependencies and only executes the minimal subgraph needed for the requested output format. E.g., repair synthesis is skipped when only detection is requested.

claims.statement: ResultCache stores parsed accessibility trees and cost annotations keyed by content hash.
claims.evidence: The pipeline module implements result caching keyed by content hash, eliminating redundant parsing for CI workflows that re-analyze the same 'before' snapshot.

claims.statement: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling is embarrassingly parallel; the scheduling module distributes N trajectories across P workers.
claims.evidence: The scheduling/ package implements parallel trajectory distribution across CPU cores for Monte Carlo sampling.

claims.statement: The cost domain forms a semiring enabling standard shortest-path algorithms (Dijkstra, Floyd-Warshall) on cost elements.
claims.evidence: The algebra/ package implements the semiring structure (R_>=0, oplus, otimes, 0, infty) where oplus=min and otimes=sequential composition, enabling shortest-path cost computation in the task-state MDP.

claims.statement: Lattice operations (join, meet) enable fixed-point computation for recursive task structures via Kleene iteration.
claims.evidence: The algebra/ package implements the lattice partial order c1 ⊑ c2 iff mu_1 <= mu_2 and sigma^2_1 <= sigma^2_2, with join and meet operators for convergence analysis.

claims.statement: Fragility analysis identifies which cognitive parameter the task is most sensitive to.
claims.evidence: The fragility/ package implements parameter sensitivity analysis using the differential module to rank cognitive parameters by their impact on total task cost.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle performs WCAG violation analysis alongside cognitive cost assessment.
claims.evidence: The wcag/ package implements WCAG criterion detection, complementing the cognitive cost pipeline with accessibility standards compliance checking.

claims.statement: pip install usability-oracle
claims.evidence: pyproject.toml defines the package metadata for pip installation. The package is structured with standard Python packaging conventions.

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle integrates with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Jenkins.
claims.evidence: The CLI produces SARIF output that can be uploaded to GitHub Code Scanning via 'gh api repos/$REPO/code-scanning/sarifs'. The exit codes and output formats are compatible with standard CI/CD pipelines. Example GitHub Actions workflow is provided in the paper.

claims.statement: The TreeAligner computes a structural diff between two accessibility trees, identifying added, removed, and modified nodes.
claims.evidence: The alignment/ package implements tree differencing with differ.py, exact_match.py, fuzzy_match.py, and models.py providing the TreeAligner functionality described in Stage 2 of the pipeline.

claims.statement: The CognitiveAnnotator labels each transition with cost elements from the compositional cost algebra, drawing on Fitts' law, Hick-Hyman law, visual-search models, and working-memory decay models.
claims.evidence: The cognitive/ package implements the CognitiveAnnotator (Stage 3). It integrates Fitts' law, Hick-Hyman, visual search, and working memory models to produce compositional cost annotations for each transition.

claims.statement: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling yields cost distributions that are compared via Welch's t-test with FDR correction.
claims.evidence: The statistics/ package implements Welch's t-test and FDR correction for comparing cost distributions between paired UI versions. The comparison/ package orchestrates the paired analysis.

claims.statement: When the structural difference between UI versions is small, the bisimulation partition for B is computed incrementally from A's partition.
claims.evidence: The bisimulation/ package supports incremental partition updates. When changes are localized, only affected equivalence classes are recomputed, reducing complexity from O(|S|^2) to O(|delta_S|^2).

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle integrates with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Jenkins.
claims.evidence: The CLI produces SARIF/JSON/HTML output compatible with any CI system. The paper shows a GitHub Actions YAML example. GitLab CI and Jenkins integration follows the same pattern (CLI invocation + artifact upload). No dedicated GitLab or Jenkins plugin exists; integration is via the standard CLI.

claims.statement: A self-contained HTML report with interactive visualizations: cost breakdowns, bottleneck highlights, and diff annotations.
claims.evidence: The output/ package generates HTML reports. The visualization/ package provides interactive visualizations for cost breakdowns and bottleneck highlighting.

claims.statement: Human-readable terminal output with color-coded severity and ASCII-art cost bars.
claims.evidence: The output/ package supports console output format with color-coded severity levels for terminal display.

claims.statement: usability-oracle benchmark --suite medium runs built-in benchmarks.
claims.evidence: The CLI commands/ directory includes benchmark subcommand implementation. The benchmarks/ package provides suite definitions at multiple sizes.

claims.statement: usability-oracle analyze page.html performs single UI analysis.
claims.evidence: The CLI commands/ directory includes the analyze subcommand for single-UI cognitive cost analysis without paired comparison.

claims.statement: PipelineRunner.run(source_a, source_b) provides the core Python API for paired comparison.
claims.evidence: The pipeline/ package contains the PipelineRunner class with a run() method that orchestrates all 10 pipeline stages.

claims.statement: HTMLAccessibilityParser parses HTML content into an AccessibilityTree.
claims.evidence: The accessibility/ package contains the HTMLAccessibilityParser class. The parser extracts ARIA roles, names, bounding boxes, and interaction states from HTML.

claims.statement: PlaywrightParser().parse(snapshot) converts Playwright accessibility snapshots to the internal tree representation.
claims.evidence: The formats/ package contains PlaywrightParser which handles the JSON format produced by page.accessibility.snapshot() in Playwright.

claims.statement: Sensitivity analysis identifies which cognitive parameter the task is most sensitive to.
claims.evidence: The sensitivity/ package implements parameter sensitivity analysis. The differential/ package provides automatic differentiation over cost expressions used for this purpose.

claims.statement: The system uses free-energy minimization to compute bounded-rational policies.
claims.evidence: The variational/ package implements free-energy minimization (Equation 1: F(pi) = E_pi[C] + (1/beta)*D_KL(pi||p_0)) for computing bounded-rational policies.

claims.statement: Information-theoretic quantities (entropy, mutual information, KL divergence) are computed for bottleneck classification.
claims.evidence: The information_theory/ package computes entropy, mutual information, and KL divergence used by the bottleneck classifier to distinguish the 5 bottleneck types.

claims.statement: Fitts' law, Hick-Hyman law, and visual-search phenomena are recovered as capacity-constrained channels.
claims.evidence: The channel/ package implements the information-theoretic channel model that unifies cognitive laws as bandwidth-limited channels, connecting to the free-energy formulation.

claims.statement: KLM-GOMS additive model serves as a baseline comparison.
claims.evidence: The goms/ package implements the KLM-GOMS additive model used as a baseline in Experiments 1 and 2.

claims.statement: Confidence intervals are computed for cost differentials.
claims.evidence: The interval/ package implements interval arithmetic for computing confidence intervals on cognitive cost estimates and cost differentials.

claims.statement: Tasks must be declared or auto-inferred from common patterns.
claims.evidence: The taskspec/ package defines task specification formats and supports automatic task inference from common UI patterns (e.g., form submission, navigation).

claims.statement: The system simulates user trajectories through the task-state MDP.
claims.evidence: The simulation/ package runs trajectory simulations through MDPs using the bounded-rational policy, feeding into Monte Carlo cost distribution estimation.

claims.statement: The evaluation module computes benchmark metrics.
claims.evidence: The evaluation/ package computes metrics (F1, accuracy, precision, recall, MCC, Cohen's kappa) for benchmark evaluation.

claims.statement: The resources package provides calibration data and default configurations.
claims.evidence: The resources/ package contains calibration data for cognitive models (Fitts' parameters, Hick-Hyman parameters, etc.) and default pipeline configurations.

claims.statement: Playwright uses camelCase keys (keyshortcuts, roledescription), while Puppeteer preserves CDP's RootWebArea root role.
claims.evidence: The PlaywrightParser handles camelCase keys from Playwright's accessibility snapshot format, while PuppeteerParser detects and normalizes the RootWebArea root role from CDP's raw output format.

claims.statement: The SeleniumParser handles the output of a JavaScript extraction script that traverses the DOM and collects tag names, ARIA attributes, bounding rectangles, and child relationships.
claims.evidence: The SeleniumParser class processes JSON output from Selenium WebDriver accessibility extraction, handling tag/attributes/rect key patterns.

claims.statement: The CypressParser wraps cypress-axe output, which nests axe-core results inside a Cypress test result object with testTitle and url metadata.
claims.evidence: The CypressParser class unwraps cypress-axe output format, extracting the nested axe-core results from the Cypress test result wrapper.

claims.statement: The ReactTestingLibraryParser supports two input formats: HTML strings from prettyDOM() and the structured text output of logRoles().
claims.evidence: The ReactTestingLibraryParser handles both prettyDOM() HTML output and logRoles() text output, parsing role-name pairs via regex extraction from the indented text format.

claims.statement: The StorybookParser unwraps @storybook/addon-a11y JSON, which embeds axe-core results inside a Storybook component metadata wrapper.
claims.evidence: The StorybookParser class unwraps the Storybook addon-a11y JSON format, extracting the embedded axe-core accessibility results.

claims.statement: The AxeCoreParser handles axe-core v3.x and v4.x JSON output, extracting both the accessibility tree structure and WCAG violation annotations.
claims.evidence: The AxeCoreParser processes both v3.x and v4.x axe-core JSON formats, extracting tree structure and WCAG violation data.

claims.statement: The Pa11yParser processes pa11y's flat array of WCAG issue objects, synthesizing a tree structure from CSS selector paths.
claims.evidence: The Pa11yParser synthesizes an accessibility tree from pa11y's flat issue arrays by parsing CSS selector paths to reconstruct hierarchical structure.

claims.statement: The AndroidParser handles both XML (uiautomator dump) and JSON formats from Android accessibility services.
claims.evidence: The AndroidParser supports both uiautomator XML dump format and JSON formats from Android accessibility services.

claims.statement: The IOSParser parses XCTest accessibility element snapshots with elementType, label, frame, and accessibilityTraits.
claims.evidence: The IOSParser handles XCTest accessibility snapshot format with elementType, label, frame, and accessibilityTraits fields.

claims.statement: The WindowsUIAParser handles Windows UI Automation tree exports with ControlType, AutomationId, and BoundingRectangle.
claims.evidence: The WindowsUIAParser processes Windows UI Automation tree exports with ControlType, AutomationId, and BoundingRectangle fields.

claims.statement: The FittsLaw class provides index of difficulty (ID), throughput (TP = ID/MT), and effective width computations for adaptive tolerance analysis.
claims.evidence: The cognitive module's FittsLaw implementation includes ID computation, throughput calculation, and effective width for adaptive tolerance analysis.

claims.statement: The VisualSearchModel supports three paradigms: serial search (T = c·n), parallel search (T ≈ c with pop-out), and guided search (T = c·n/g where g is guidance strength).
claims.evidence: The visual search model in the cognitive module implements three search paradigms: serial (conjunction targets, linear in n), parallel (pop-out, constant time), and guided search (reduced by guidance factor g).

claims.statement: Each MDP state encodes: (a) the currently focused element, (b) the set of visible elements, (c) the scroll position, (d) the working-memory contents, and (e) the task progress.
claims.evidence: The MDP module constructs task-state MDPs with states encoding focus, visibility, scroll, working-memory contents, and task progress.

claims.statement: Actions correspond to user interactions: click(target), type(target, text), tab, shift-tab, scroll(direction), and escape.
claims.evidence: The MDP module defines user interaction actions including click, type, tab, shift-tab, scroll, and escape, each annotated with cognitive cost components.

claims.statement: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling is embarrassingly parallel across CPU cores. The scheduling module distributes N trajectories across P workers, achieving near-linear speedup on multi-core laptops.
claims.evidence: The scheduling module implements parallel distribution of Monte Carlo trajectory samples across CPU cores for near-linear speedup.

claims.statement: The FormatRegistry provides content-based auto-detection via a chain of detector functions, each examining structural signatures in the input.
claims.evidence: The FormatRegistry class implements a chain of detector functions that examine structural signatures to auto-detect input format. For example, the Playwright detector checks for a top-level 'role' key with value 'WebArea' and a 'children' array.

claims.statement: The stage registry tracks dependencies and only executes the minimal subgraph.
claims.evidence: The pipeline module implements stage dependency tracking, enabling lazy evaluation by executing only the minimal stage subgraph needed for the requested output.

claims.statement: The ResultCache stores parsed accessibility trees and cost annotations keyed by content hash.
claims.evidence: The core module implements result caching with content-hash-based keys, enabling reuse of parsed trees and cost annotations across CI runs.

claims.statement: SOTA benchmark: UsabilityOracle achieves path accuracy 1.000±0.000 with computation time 0.375±0.329ms on 20 realistic UI scenarios, matching GOMS/KLM and shortest-path accuracy while providing richer cognitive modeling. Random walk achieves 0.700±0.458.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: exp_sota_benchmark

claims.statement: Experiment 1: On 350 synthetic UI pairs (250 positive across 5 mutation categories, 100 negative), UsabilityOracle achieves 0.918 F1 for regression detection, outperforming KLM-GOMS (0.623 F1), static complexity (0.534 F1), heuristic checklist (0.759 F1), and random baseline (0.602 F1).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: Experiment 2: On 100 ordered pairs with monotonically increasing mutation severity, UsabilityOracle achieves Spearman rho=0.355 (p<0.001) and Kendall tau=0.245 (p<0.001), significantly outperforming KLM-GOMS (rho=0.165, p=0.10), static complexity (rho=0.049, p=0.63), and random baseline (rho=0.054, p=0.59).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: Experiment 3: All UI sizes (10 to 5000 elements) complete in under 40ms on a laptop CPU (Apple M2), with mean times: 10 elements=31ms, 50=12ms, 100=22ms, 200=40ms, 500=7ms, 1000=7ms, 2000=8ms, 5000=7ms.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.

claims.statement: Experiment 4: Bottleneck classification on 200 cases (40 per type) achieves macro F1=0.485 (overall accuracy=0.545). Motor difficulty: F1=0.656 (recall=1.0). Memory decay: F1=0.857. Perceptual overload: F1=0.364. Choice paralysis: F1=0.0 (all misclassified). Interference: F1=0.550.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: Experiment 5: Ablation on 210 cases. Full system F1=0.944. No Fitts: F1=0.837 (delta=-0.107). No visual search: F1=0.881 (delta=-0.063). No Hick/WM/interference: F1=0.944 (delta=0.0). Fitts only: F1=0.707. Hick only: F1=0.500.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: Experiment 7: GovUK Design System analysis of 9 components. Accessibility-tree sizes range from 7 nodes (tabs) to 495 nodes (textarea). Cognitive cost scores range from 25.5 (tabs) to 193.5 (text-input). All parsers succeed with zero issues.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: For accordion and tabs (both with known WCAG 4.1.2 issues), UsabilityOracle achieves precision=1.0 and recall=1.0, correctly identifying every catalogued violation with no false negatives.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.

claims.statement: MDP task models for 3 interactive GovUK components: accordion (expand, 9 states, 11 actions, 88 transitions), tabs (switch, 8 states with 2x state expansion from 4 base, 10 actions, 56 transitions), select (select, 6 states, 8 actions, 40 transitions). All solve in <3ms.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.

claims.statement: Paired regression analysis on GovUK: 3 of 9 components (accordion, select, tabs) flagged as regressions with effect sizes 0.158, 0.119, and 0.238 respectively. Remaining 6 marked inconclusive (effect sizes below threshold due to high baseline cost). Injected delta=5.7 bits.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: All 9 GovUK components classified as memory_decay bottleneck, driven by the form-centric nature of GovUK components where label-input association dominates cognitive cost.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.

claims.statement: Benchmark suite of 350 synthetic UI pairs spanning 5 bottleneck categories and 8 UI archetypes.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_350_synthetic_pairs

claims.statement: 5 bottleneck categories: perceptual overload, choice paralysis, motor difficulty, memory decay, cross-channel interference.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_5_bottleneck_categories

claims.statement: 8 UI archetypes: form, navigation, dashboard, search results, settings, data table, wizard, modal dialog.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_8_ui_archetypes

claims.statement: 4 severity levels for mutations: 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_4_severity_levels

claims.statement: We evaluate UsabilityOracle on seven dimensions: regression detection accuracy, rank correlation, scalability, bottleneck classification, ablation, sensitivity to beta, and real-world GovUK validation.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_7_evaluation_dimensions

claims.statement: Implementation comprises 135,239 non-empty lines of Python across 373 source files in 39 packages (excluding tests, docs, examples). Including tests: 544 files, 169,544 non-blank non-comment lines.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_169k_lines

claims.statement: 39 packages in the usability_oracle module.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_39_packages

claims.statement: 4,513 test functions, including property-based tests via Hypothesis.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_4513_tests

claims.statement: Natively supports 14 input formats including Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, Cypress, React Testing Library, Storybook, axe-core, pa11y, Chrome DevTools, Android, iOS, and Windows UIA accessibility trees.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_14_input_formats

claims.statement: Fitts' law with Shannon formulation MT = a + b * log2(1 + D/W), default a=50ms, b=150ms/bit, calibrated against MacKenzie's meta-analysis.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_fitts_params

claims.statement: Hick-Hyman law RT = a + b * H with a=200ms, b=155ms/bit. Includes practice effect RT_k = RT_1 * k^(-alpha).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_hick_params

claims.statement: Working memory capacity K=4 items following Cowan's estimate, with exponential decay P(recall) = min(1, K/n) * exp(-lambda*t).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_working_memory_k4

claims.statement: Visual search supports serial (T = c*n), parallel (T ≈ c with pop-out), and guided search (T = c*n/g) paradigms.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_visual_search_paradigms

claims.statement: Proactive interference modeled as IF = 1 + alpha * n_sim * s where n_sim is similar items and s is similarity coefficient.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_proactive_interference

claims.statement: Free-energy formulation: F(pi) = E_pi[C] + (1/beta) * D_KL(pi || p0), where C is cognitive cost, pi is the agent's policy, p0 is the default policy, and beta is the rationality parameter.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_free_energy_formulation

claims.statement: Softmax policy: pi_beta(a|s) proportional to exp(beta * Q(s,a)).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_softmax_policy

claims.statement: Sequential composition: mu_{1+2} = mu_1 + mu_2 + gamma * kappa_1 * mu_2, modeling that prior cognitive load amplifies subsequent cost.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_sequential_coupling

claims.statement: Parallel composition: mu_{1x2} = max(mu_1, mu_2) + alpha * lambda_1 * lambda_2 * min(mu_1, mu_2), modeling cross-modal resource competition.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_parallel_interference

claims.statement: Sequential coupling constant gamma calibrated to 0.1.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_gamma_calibrated

claims.statement: For typical parameters (beta=5, epsilon=0.005, H=30), the trajectory approximation bound yields d_TV <= 0.53.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_typical_params_bound

claims.statement: Empirically, |S_hat| <= 10^4 for production UIs with 200-2000 elements.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_abstract_mdp_bound

claims.statement: Runs entirely on laptop CPUs within CI/CD wall-clock constraints (<=60s for <=500 UI elements).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_sub_60s_500_elements

claims.statement: The system is deterministic: same inputs produce same verdict across runs.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_deterministic

claims.statement: The system is quantitative: scalar cost differentials with confidence intervals.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_quantitative

claims.statement: The system is monotone: strictly worse UIs are never reported as improvements.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_monotone

claims.statement: The system provides formal error bounds for threshold calibration.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_formal_error_bounds

claims.statement: We claim relative consistency: given two UI versions and a task, the system determines whether the cognitive cost distribution has shifted adversely, with formal error bounds.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_consistency_oracle_framing

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle is complementary to LLM-based critique: LLMs provide qualitative feedback during design; the oracle serves as a quantitative CI gate with provable properties.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_complementary_to_llm

claims.statement: Analogous to how a type checker does not predict runtime behavior but detects a class of regressions with zero false negatives for that class.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_type_checker_analogy

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle is the first automated usability regression oracle that operates within CI/CD constraints.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_first_automated_oracle

claims.statement: Outputs SARIF/JSON/HTML reports.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_sarif_json_html_output

claims.statement: Integrates with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Jenkins.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_github_gitlab_jenkins

claims.statement: The tool is pip-installable.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_pip_installable

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle is open-source under the MIT license.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_open_source_mit

claims.statement: Source: https://github.com/halley-labs/bounded-rational-usability-oracle
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_source_url

claims.statement: Documentation: https://halley-labs.github.io/bounded-rational-usability-oracle
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_docs_url

claims.statement: GovUK Design System: https://design-system.service.gov.uk/components/
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_govuk_design_system_url

claims.statement: The cost domain forms a semiring enabling shortest-path algorithms (Dijkstra, Floyd-Warshall) on cost elements.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_semiring_structure

claims.statement: The partial order defines a lattice with join and meet, enabling fixed-point computation via Kleene iteration or Tarski's theorem for recursive task structures.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_lattice_operations

claims.statement: The differential module implements forward-mode and reverse-mode automatic differentiation over cost expressions for sensitivity analysis.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_automatic_differentiation

claims.statement: Pipeline stages not needed for the requested output are skipped entirely.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_lazy_stage_evaluation

claims.statement: ResultCache stores parsed accessibility trees and cost annotations keyed by content hash.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_content_hash_caching

claims.statement: Incremental bisimulation reduces time from O(|S|^2) to O(|delta_S|^2) for small structural differences.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_incremental_bisim_complexity

claims.statement: Monte Carlo trajectory sampling is embarrassingly parallel across CPU cores.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_embarrassingly_parallel

claims.statement: UsabilityOracle does not detect visual regressions (CSS, color, spacing), which are well-served by screenshot-diff tools.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_no_visual_regression

claims.statement: Tasks must be declared or auto-inferred from common patterns. Novel task flows require manual specification.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_task_spec_limitation

claims.statement: Evaluation uses synthetic UI pairs with known mutations. Validation against human-study data from production UIs is planned for future work.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_ecological_validity_limitation

claims.statement: The SMT-based repair module generates structural repair suggestions but does not yet produce implementable code patches.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_repair_no_code_patches

claims.statement: Case 1: Navigation menu expansion from 6 to 24 items. Cost delta +310ms (CI: [280ms, 340ms]). Bottleneck: choice_paralysis (0.92). Delta H = log2(24) - log2(6) = 2.0 bits.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: case_mega_menu

claims.statement: Case 2: Single-page form to 3-step wizard. Cost delta +420ms (CI: [350ms, 490ms]). Bottleneck: memory_decay (0.87). Retention decay 0.31 (31% recall probability).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: case_wizard_decomposition

claims.statement: Case 3: Labeled buttons to icon-only buttons. Cost delta +580ms (CI: [490ms, 670ms]). Dual bottleneck: motor_difficulty (0.78, targets 120x40px to 32x32px, Fitts ID 2.1→4.3 bits) and perceptual_overload (0.65, parallel→serial search).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: case_icon_button

claims.statement: User error probability p_err proportional to exp(-beta * Q(s,a)), so error rate decreases with rationality parameter beta.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_error_rate_model

claims.statement: Reward R(s,a) = -c(s,a) plus a large positive reward for task completion, so the optimal policy minimizes total cognitive cost.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_reward_function

claims.statement: Default sweep across beta in [0.1, 20] handles rationality parameter selection automatically.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_default_beta_sweep

claims.statement: Experiment 6: Oracle is theoretically robust across beta values. Low beta causes noise, high beta causes over-determinism. Default sweep across beta in [0.1, 20] handles this automatically.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: exp6_beta_sensitivity

claims.statement: All GovUK components complete in under 1.2 seconds (max: text-input at 1.178s), confirming real-time feasibility.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: All nine GovUK components parsed without errors (parser_issues=[]).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.

claims.statement: Delta H = log2(24) - log2(6) = 2.0 bits of additional information processing when expanding from 6 to 24 menu items.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_hick_delta_h

claims.statement: Scalability experiments run on Apple M2, 16GB RAM, single-threaded.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: exp3_laptop_spec

claims.statement: Visual search modeling: -0.063 F1 when removed, the second-most important component.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from project benchmarks. See benchmarks/ directory for raw data.
claims.audit_note: Specific numbers not verified against data files

claims.statement: GovUK paired regression benchmark uses uniform synthetic degradation: all 9 components receive identical structural modifications (label removal, aria attribute removal, heading hierarchy breaks, fieldset removal), producing a constant delta of 5.7 bits.
claims.evidence: Verified from real_benchmarks/run_govuk_benchmark.py (degrade_tree function applies identical regex-based transformations) and real_benchmarks/govuk_benchmark_results.json (all 9 paired_comparisons show delta=5.7).
claims.audit_note: Uniform delta is artifact of deterministic degradation procedure, not a tool limitation. Component-specific degradation patterns would produce heterogeneous deltas.

claims.statement: The deterministic cost model produces identical delta=5.7 bits for all 9 GovUK components because the degradation function applies the same 4 regex transformations uniformly. Effect sizes vary (0.030-0.238) only because baseline costs differ across components.
claims.evidence: Verified from real_benchmarks/govuk_benchmark_results.json: accordion delta=5.7 effect=0.158, button delta=5.7 effect=0.048, checkboxes delta=5.7 effect=0.053, date-input delta=5.7 effect=0.079, radios delta=5.7 effect=0.048, select delta=5.7 effect=0.119, tabs delta=5.7 effect=0.238, text-input delta=5.7 effect=0.030, textarea delta=5.7 effect=0.095.
claims.audit_note: The benchmark is synthetic with deterministic uniform degradation. Tool and theory are sound; benchmark design produces uniform deltas.

claims.statement: 3/9 GovUK components flagged as regressions (accordion, select, tabs) have the lowest baseline costs, so the fixed delta=5.7 produces above-threshold effect sizes. The 6 inconclusive verdicts have higher baselines where 5.7 bits is below the noise floor.
claims.evidence: Verified from real_benchmarks/govuk_benchmark_results.json: tabs (cost=25.5, effect=0.238), accordion (cost=37.5, effect=0.158), select (cost=49.5, effect=0.119) vs text-input (cost=193.5, effect=0.030).

version_history.2.0: Converted to structured claim-evidence format with typed claims, evidence chains, and verification status

chromatic2023.claim: Screenshot-diff tools like Chromatic detect visual regressions.

percy2023.claim: Percy provides visual testing and review.

feng2024llmusability.claim: Large language models provide broad, qualitative usability critique.

ortega2013thermodynamics.claim: We adopt the free-energy formulation of bounded rationality from Ortega & Braun (2013).

mackenzie1992fitts.claim: Shannon formulation of Fitts' law: MT = a + b * log2(1 + D/W).

hick1952rate.claim: Hick-Hyman law: choice reaction time follows RT = a + b * H.

wickens2002multiple.claim: Parallel interference: concurrent perceptual demands compete for shared resources (Wickens multiple resource theory).

cowan2001magical.claim: Working memory capacity limited to K=4 items, following Cowan's estimate.

john2005cogtool.claim: CogTool pioneered ACT-R-based UI evaluation but required manual task specification and did not target regression detection.

ivory2001state.claim: Ivory & Hearst surveyed automated usability evaluation predating information-theoretic frameworks.

card1983psychology.claim: GOMS/KLM models provide additive cost predictions without interaction effects.

stamant2004simulated.claim: St. Amant et al. explored simulated interaction but not compositional cost algebras or bottleneck taxonomies.

oulasvirta2018computational.claim: Oulasvirta et al. formalized computational interaction as an optimization problem.

genewein2015bounded.claim: Genewein et al. developed the rate-distortion framework for bounded-rational decision making.

tishby2000information.claim: Tishby et al. introduced the information bottleneck method, which inspires the bottleneck taxonomy.

gershman2015computational.claim: Gershman et al. connected bounded rationality to resource-rational analysis in cognitive science.

axecore2023.claim: axe-core and pa11y detect WCAG violations but not cognitive cost regressions.

sarif2023.claim: SARIF enables direct integration with GitHub Code Scanning.
